nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Wölpe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Ethiopian: from the personal name Wolde, which is interpreted as ‘my son’ in the Ge’ez or Amharic languages. It often forms part of a Christian name, such as Woldemariam , and in this case it translates as ‘son of’. — Note: Since Ethiopians do not have hereditary surnames, this name was registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.

North German: habitational name from either of two places so named in Mecklenburg or Pomerania or from Wolda in Lower Saxony. Possibly also a topographic name from an inflected form of Middle Low German wolt, walt ‘woods’.

variants.name: Woldemariam
meanings: Ethiopian (also Wolde Mariam): from the male personal name Wolde Mariam, which has the Christian religious meaning ‘son of Mary’ (see Wolde and Mariam ). — Note: Since Ethiopians do not have hereditary surnames, this name was registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.

variants.name: Woldemichael
meanings: Ethiopian (also Wolde Michael): from the personal name Wolde Michael, which has the Christian religious meaning ‘son of St. Michael’ (see Wolde and Michael ). — Note: Since Ethiopians do not have hereditary surnames, this name was registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.

nameMeanings.name: Eilbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from gisil ‘pledge’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. This was the name of the founder of the only native British religious order (abolished at the Dissolution of the Monasteries), St Gilbert of Sempringham ( ?1083–1189 ), in whose honour it is still sometimes bestowed, especially among Roman Catholics. It gained a wider currency in the 19th century.

en.lifeSummary: Graf Eilbert von Wölpe I was born about 1090, in Wölpe, Erichshagen, Nienburg, Hanover, Prussia, Germany as the son of Ludwig von Wölpe. He had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with Adelheid von Wassel. He died in 1167, at the age of 78.

ko.lifeSummary: Graf Eilbert von Wölpe I(은)는 대략 1090년 독일 프레우ss 프로빈츠 해노버 니버그 에리크샤겐 wo프에서 Ludwig von Wölpe의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Adelheid von Wassel(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1167년 78세의 나이로 사망했다.
